title: Fermented Oozey Dutch Herring Pickle
description: A velvety, nostalgic Dutch dish featuring fermented herring, perfect for spherifying and serving as a Jain-friendly snack or side. This recipe combines traditional Dutch flavors with a modern twist, resulting in a deliciously unique pickled herring.

ingredients.name: Herring fillets (500g)
ingredients.notes: Ferment 24 hours before serving, or to taste.

ingredients.name: Sorghum vinegar
ingredients.notes: Replace with other vinegar if unavailable.

ingredients.name: Mustard seeds
ingredients.notes: Black or brown, or omit for vegan version.

ingredients.name: Sea salt
ingredients.notes: Adjust to taste, reduce for vegan version.

instructions: Combine herring fillets, sorghum vinegar, ginger, garlic, mustard seeds, coriander seeds, caraway seeds, and sea salt in a glass container with a lid. Massage the mixture with your hands until the herring is evenly coated.
Transfer the container to a warm, dark place and allow the mixture to ferment for 24 hours, stirring occasionally.
Strain the liquid from the fermentation mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bowl, discarding the solids.
Add vegetable oil to the strained liquid and stir to combine.
For spherification, use sodium alginate and calcium chloride, as per your spherification method of choice, adjusting the amount of liquid accordingly.
Place the resulting pickled herring spheres onto a serving dish and garnish with fresh dill, to taste.
Serve immediately, or store in the refrigerator for up to 1 week, allowing to come to room temperature before serving.
